lesson_name: Corrective Training: DATA Line Completion + Core Pattern Consistency
description: Targets 4 failure patterns: (1) truncated DATA lines at end of output, (2) consistent variable access patterns, (3) rotating/physics DeltaSeconds wiring, (4) multi-step DATA chains. All examples emphasize complete DATA connection lines as the final section of DSL output.

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with a boolean variable IsActive. On BeginPlay, check if IsActive is true. If true, print Active. If false, print Inactive.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_BranchCheck
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsActive: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsActive]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Active"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Inactive"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n4.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with a boolean variable DoorOpen defaulting to false. On overlap, if DoorOpen is false, print Opening Door and set DoorOpen to true.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DoorCheck
PARENT: Actor

VAR DoorOpen: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=DoorOpen]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Opening Door"]
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=DoorOpen, Value=true]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with an integer Score variable set to 0. On BeginPlay, check if Score is greater than 100. If true print Winner, if false print Keep Playing.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ScoreCheck
PARENT: Actor

VAR Score: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n3: GreaterThan [B=100]
NODE n4: Branch
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Winner"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Keep Playing"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.True -> n5.Execute
EXEC n4.False -> n6.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay calculates (5 + 3) multiplied by 2 and prints the result.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_MathCalc
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: AddFloat [A=5.0, B=3.0]
NODE n3: MultiplyFloat [B=2.0]
NODE n4: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay subtracts 25 from a Health variable of 100, then prints the result.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_HealthMath
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat [B=25.0]
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay divides 100 by 3, clamps the result between 0 and 50, and prints it.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ClampMath
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: DivideFloat [A=100.0, B=3.0]
NODE n3: ClampFloat [Min=0.0, Max=50.0]
NODE n4: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with an actor array called Targets. On BeginPlay, loop through each actor and destroy it.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DestroyTargets
PARENT: Actor

VAR Targets: Array<Actor>

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Targets]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n4.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.Target [Actor]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with an actor array Enemies. On BeginPlay loop through each enemy, get its distance, and if closer than 500 units print Nearby Enemy.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_NearbyCheck
PARENT: Actor

VAR Enemies: Array<Actor>

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Enemies]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDistanceTo
NODE n5: LessThan [B=500.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Nearby Enemy"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.OtherActor [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint for an actor that continuously rotates at 90 degrees per second on the Yaw axis.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_RotatingActor
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: MakeRotator [Yaw=90.0]
NODE n3: MultiplyFloat
NODE n4: AddActorLocalRotation

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute

DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.DeltaRotation [Rotator]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that rotates an actor at 45 degrees per second on the Pitch axis using Event Tick.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PitchRotator
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: MakeRotator [Pitch=45.0]
NODE n3: MultiplyFloat
NODE n4: AddActorLocalRotation

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute

DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.DeltaRotation [Rotator]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that on Tick moves an actor forward by 200 units per second using AddActorLocalOffset.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_MoveForward
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetActorForwardVector
NODE n3: MultiplyFloat [B=200.0]
NODE n4: AddActorLocalOffset

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute

DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n4.DeltaLocation [Vector]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.DeltaLocation [Vector]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that on overlap, casts the other actor to Character. If cast succeeds, get the character's name and print it. If cast fails, print Unknown Actor.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CastAndPrint
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: GetDisplayName
NODE n4: PrintString
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Unknown Actor"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.CastFailed -> n5.Execute

DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
DATA n2.AsCharacter -> n3.Object [Actor]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that on overlap casts to Character. If the cast succeeds, subtract 10 from a Health variable and print the remaining health.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CastDamage
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n4: SubtractFloat [B=10.0]
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n6: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute

DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
DATA n3.Value -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Value [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n6.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that on AnyDamage subtracts the damage from Health variable, prints Took X damage where X is the damage amount, then checks if health is below zero and prints Dead if so.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DamageHandler
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n5: PrintString
NODE n6: LessThan [B=0.0]
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="Dead"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.True -> n8.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n5.InString [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that on AnyDamage gets the damage amount, multiplies it by 1.5, subtracts from Health, and if Health is less than 25 print Critical Health.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CriticalDamage
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: MultiplyFloat [B=1.5]
NODE n3: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n4: SubtractFloat
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n6: LessThan [B=25.0]
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="Critical Health"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.True -> n8.Execute

DATA n1.Damage -> n2.A [Float]
DATA n3.Value -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n4.B [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Value [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with boolean IsVisible defaulting to true. On pressing Toggle, use NotBool to flip IsVisible, save the new value, and call SetActorHiddenInGame.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ToggleVis
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsVisible: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Toggle"]
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsVisible]
NODE n3: NotBool
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=IsVisible]
NODE n5: SetActorHiddenInGame

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Input [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.bNewHidden [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint that when Fire input is pressed, gets the actor's location and rotation, and spawns a BP_Projectile at that transform.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ProjectileSpawner
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Fire"]
NODE n2: GetActorLocation
NODE n3: GetActorRotation
NODE n4: SpawnActorFromClass [ActorClass=BP_Projectile]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n4.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n4.SpawnLocation [Vector]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.SpawnRotation [Rotator]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with a Gate node. On pressing Open input, open the gate and print Gate Opened. On pressing Close input, close the gate and print Gate Closed. On Tick, enter the gate, and the exit prints Active.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateStatus
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Open"]
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Gate Opened"]
NODE n4: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Close"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Gate Closed"]
NODE n6: Event_Tick
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Active"]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n2.Open
EXEC n1.Pressed -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Pressed -> n2.Close
EXEC n4.Pressed -> n5.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n7.Execute

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with float variables Health and MaxHealth (100). On AnyDamage, subtract damage from Health, clamp between 0 and MaxHealth, save it, check if less than or equal to 0, if so print Dead and destroy the actor.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_FullHealth
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0
VAR MaxHealth: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: GetVar [Variable=MaxHealth]
NODE n4: SubtractFloat
NODE n5: ClampFloat [Min=0.0]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n7: LessEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n8: Branch
NODE n9: PrintString [InString="Dead"]
NODE n10: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.True -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n4.B [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Value [Float]
DATA n3.Value -> n5.Max [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n7.A [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with an actor array Pickups and integer Score set to 0. On BeginPlay, loop through each pickup, add 10 to Score for each one, destroy the pickup, then after the loop print the total Score.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CollectAll
PARENT: Actor

VAR Pickups: Array<Actor>
VAR Score: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Pickups]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n5: AddFloat [B=10]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n7: DestroyActor
NODE n8: GetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n9: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n3.Completed -> n9.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Int]
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n7.Target [Actor]
DATA n8.Value -> n9.InString [String]
